alternative_parents.description: Purine nucleotides bound to a saccharide derivative through the terminal phosphate group.
alternative_parents.name: Purine nucleotide sugars

alternative_parents.description: Purine ribobucleotides with diphosphate group linked to the ribose moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Purine ribonucleoside diphosphates

alternative_parents.description: Purine ribobucleotides with one phosphate group attached to 2' and 5' hydroxyl groups of the ribose moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Purine ribonucleoside 2',5'-bisphosphates

alternative_parents.description: Pyridine nucleotides, in which the pyridine base is nicotinamide or a derivative thereof.
alternative_parents.name: Nicotinamide nucleotides

alternative_parents.description: Carbohydrate derivatives containing a pentose substituted by one or more phosphate groups.
alternative_parents.name: Pentose phosphates

alternative_parents.description: Compounds consisting of an amine with a beta-N-glycosidic bond to a carbohydrate, thus forming a cyclic hemiaminal ether bond (alpha-amino ether).
alternative_parents.name: Glycosylamines

alternative_parents.description: Purines that carry an amino group at position 6. Purine is a bicyclic aromatic compound made up of a pyrimidine ring fused to an imidazole ring.
alternative_parents.name: 6-aminopurines

alternative_parents.description: Monosaccharides comprising a phosphated group linked to the carbohydrate unit.
alternative_parents.name: Monosaccharide phosphates

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the pyrophosphate oxoanion, with the structure OP([O-])(=O)OP(O)([O-])=O.
alternative_parents.name: Organic pyrophosphates

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic aromatic compounds containing a pyridine ring substituted at position 3 by a carboxamide group.
alternative_parents.name: Nicotinamides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an amino group attached to a pyrimidine ring. Pyrimidine is a 6-membered ring consisting of four carbon atoms and two nitrogen centers at the 1- and 3- ring positions.
alternative_parents.name: Aminopyrimidines and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a phosphate group that is linked to exactly one alkyl chain.
alternative_parents.name: Monoalkyl phosphates

alternative_parents.description: Cyclic organooxygen compounds containing the structure RC(=N)N where the central carbon atom and one of the linked nitrogen atoms are part of the same ring( R here is an organyl group). They can also be viewed as analogs of lactams where the oxygen atom is replaced by a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Imidolactams

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a pyridinium ring, which is the cationic form of pyridine.
alternative_parents.name: Pyridinium derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic compounds containing an imidazole ring substituted at position 1.
alternative_parents.name: N-substituted imidazoles

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic compounds containing a saturated, aliphatic, five-membered ring where a carbon is replaced by an oxygen.
alternative_parents.name: Tetrahydrofurans

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an amine group, which is indirectly attached to a carbonyl via an intervening vinyl (>C=C<) moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Vinylogous amides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an aromatic ring where a carbon atom is linked to an hetero atom.
alternative_parents.name: Heteroaromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds comprising primary carboxylic acid amide functional group, with the general structure RC(=O)NH2.
alternative_parents.name: Primary carboxylic acid amides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an amine group, a carboxylic acid group (or a derivative thereof), and a side-chain that is specific to each amino acid.
alternative_parents.name: Amino acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a secondary alcohol functional group, with the general structure HOC(R)(R') (R,R'=alkyl, aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Secondary alcohols

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one oxygen atom linked to a ring carbon.
alternative_parents.name: Oxacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Amines having the nitrogen atom linked to exactly one hydrocarbyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Primary amines

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a sodium ion.
alternative_parents.name: Organic sodium salts

alternative_parents.description: Organic neutral compounds having formal unit electrical charges of opposite sign.
alternative_parents.name: Organic zwitterions

class.description: Dinucleotides where the two bases are connected via a (5'->5')-phosphodiester linkage.
class.name: (5'->5')-dinucleotides

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as (5'->5')-dinucleotides. These are dinucleotides where the two bases are connected via a (5'->5')-phosphodiester linkage.

direct_parent.description: Dinucleotides where the two bases are connected via a (5'->5')-phosphodiester linkage.
direct_parent.name: (5'->5')-dinucleotides

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

superclass.description: Compounds containing a nucleobase linked to a ribose or deoxyribose sugar via a beta-glycosidic linkage. The ribose or deoxyribose moiety bears at least a phosphate group in case of nucleotides.
superclass.name: Nucleosides, nucleotides, and analogues
